findings.criterion: No core differ/apply module is modified by this story — verified by `git diff --name-only` touching only `_backend.py`, `adapters/jira/backend.py`, `adapters/jira/identity.py`, and files under `tests/`.
findings.detail: The current codebase contains modifications to core orchestration modules outside the AC4 whitelist: `applier.py`, `run_differs.py`, `fetcher.py`, `_attestation.py`, and `apply_inbound_records.py` all contain `select_backend` calls. Additionally, `_backend_registry.py` (a new file) is present but not in the AC4 whitelist. These modifications are internally attributed to S4 in docstrings (e.g. `run_differs.py:63` says "S4"), but the AC4 verification mechanism is `git diff --name-only` which I cannot run. From a static codebase view, core modules beyond the whitelist are modified, and the S2/S4 commit boundary cannot be confirmed without git tooling. AC4 is codebase-verifiable via git, and that verification cannot be completed here.

citations.description: Ticket status is in_progress with no comments recording a successful make lint/typecheck/test run.

findings.criterion: `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all green.
findings.detail: Cannot be verified from static file analysis alone; running make commands requires tool execution not available here. The story status is `in_progress`, so this criterion has not been attested to. No operator attestation is present in the ticket comments.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
